content_of_post_to_comment: I think that the term “daddy issues” is just another way to invalidate women who have already been through way too much. My father hurt me so deeply it’s taken my whole adult life to be okay. But now that I am okay, I am stable and functional and emotionally regulated. I have a lot of opinions about patriarchy, but I am careful not to aimlessly project. It’s just that I have seen first hand just how much damage men can do to women in the way our society is structured. I have lived it. I’ve had a father try to control my well into my twenties. It was hell. I don’t have “daddy issues”. I was a victim of abuse who was able to stabilise myself and now I feel VERY passionately about every woman also having the freedom to be safe.
comment: The phrase “daddy issues” collapses a history of coercion into a personality trait, shifting responsibility from actor to survivor. It also makes structural critique sound like projection.
